---
type: concept
created: 2026-06-15
updated: 2026-06-15
sources: ["[itu-t-l-1500-adaptation-framework](../sources/itu-t-l-1500-adaptation-framework.qmd)"]
tags: [method]
aliases:
  - "DRM"
  - "Disaster Risk Management"
---


# Disaster risk management

## Definition
Disaster risk management is a strategic approach focused on minimizing the adverse impacts of environmental hazards on communities by systematically reducing exposure and vulnerability to extreme events.

## Key Characteristics
- Strategic planning and implementation to mitigate the effects of natural and climatic hazards.
- Integration with [Climate change adaptation](climate-change-adaptation.qmd) to bolster community resilience.
- Utilizes [Information and communication technology](information-and-communication-technology.qmd) for predictive modeling, real-time monitoring, and emergency response.
- Emphasis on the protection of both physical and social infrastructure.

## Applications
- Development of early warning systems to predict extreme weather events.
- Assessing and mapping vulnerability levels in high-risk geographic zones.
- Enhancing the resilience of critical communication networks to remain operational during disasters.
- Coordinating multi-sectoral responses to mitigate the socioeconomic impacts of climate-related disruptions.

## Related Concepts
- [Climate change adaptation](climate-change-adaptation.qmd)
- [Information and Communication Technologies](information-and-communication-technology.qmd)
- [e-Adaptation](e-adaptation.qmd)

## Related Entities
None.

## Mentions in Source
- "Thus, disaster risk management and adaptation to climate change focus on reducing exposure and vulnerability, while contributing to increasing the resilience of vulnerable contexts to face the impacts of climatic events." — [ITU-T L.1500 (Adaptation Framework)](../sources/itu-t-l-1500-adaptation-framework.qmd)
